sourceMovie.title: Love & Other Drugs
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Audiences latched onto Love & Other Drugs (2010) as a drama theatrical experience: big enough for a group watch, specific enough to reward a second viewing once you know where it's headed. The drama and comedy blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Hitch
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Love & Other Drugs clicked for you, Hitch (2005) is a strong next stop: both trade in drama and comedy, with comic timing and sharper banter. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Love & Other Drugs?
faq.answer: Try Hitch, Don Juan DeMarco, New York, I Love You—each shares DNA with Love & Other Drugs while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Love & Other Drugs?
faq.answer: Love & Other Drugs (2010) is best described as Drama, Comedy, Romance. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Love & Other Drugs worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 7.0/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this drama lane.
